title: Level 484
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 484: "The Whiteout"}}
==Foreword== The Whiteout is a very quiet place that many of us find quite unknown. It's a giant environment found behind the doors of the [[Level 483|Grayscale Halls]]. Everyone can tell you that it's as comprehensible as incomprehensible. There are a lot of oddities to see around there. Despite its inhospitable nature, the level is seen as a peaceful place for wanderers to stay. Still, most of them, after entering by accident, try to find the exit as quickly as possible. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=The Whiteout.jpeg|caption=The first picture ever taken of The Whiteout.}} As seen by the ladders, tools, white paint, and other construction materials strewn all over the level, The Whiteout is a massive, white house undergoing renovation. Each area is distinct in its own way, even if the space is repetitive and straightforward. The rooms vary in terms of size, shape, and interior furnishings. Despite their striking similarities, they never seem to be the same. They resemble hallways, bathrooms, living rooms, and kitchens. Spatial and temporal abnormalities reside in some places, with objects in these spots phasing or shifting location. They are the alleged outcomes of deeds committed by The Maker, the only known residing entity on the level.
When we were first sent there in 2016 in order to map the level, we counted 52 rooms of all kinds, 21 corridors and 5 staircases. Over five years later we came back there again after reports stating that our maps were incorrect. We determined that the level now consists of 77 rooms, 35 corridors and 9 staircases. We came to the conclusion that The Whiteout is constantly increasing its size over time. Every year from that discovery, small groups of 4-7 Compass Point Team members are sent to see what changed on the level and update the map. A few mysteries still remain, like the identity of the Maker and his purpose for expanding these rooms. My team was sent there multiple times for that very reason, but we never quite got the answers we wanted. I remained, as the only one left, believing that the truth might been nearer than we think. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==Structure== {{Blurimage|file=Recording 20250823 200508-1.jpg|caption=Photo of an unfurnished room.}} The structure of The Whiteout is diverse; all the rooms are faded, as are the objects in them. Everything is composed of an unidentified, silky material that resembles quartz and is nearly soft to the touch. The level's surfaces are impervious to dirt and will progressively repair themselves after being damaged. Various avant-garde furniture pieces and miscellaneous items scattered around the space, including on the ceiling, are common in the rooms of The Whiteout. The walls frequently have holes in them that lead to nothing or other areas. Occasionally, they contain modest, commonplace stuff. Staircases of varying diameters populate the level, most of which lead to dead ends, while some lead to a ceiling room with sculptures. The photographs depict chaotic conglomerates of other levels mixed into one, making it difficult to tell what they are. Rooms despite their striking similarities, they never seem to be the same. They resemble spaces like kitchens, livingrooms, bathrooms and corridors.
==Lights== The first time entering The Whiteout is quite unpleasant for most people. There aren't many Backrooms levels with such a high intensity of light. Long-term exposure to it may result in sleep deprivation and partial blinding at worst; however, this is rarely considered a problem for most people. It all depends on how our body adapts to the intense stream of light. Some stay there for longer periods of time; others leave almost immediately. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==Sculpture rooms== Sculpture rooms are individual rooms with 1-4 sculpures placed on a pedestals. Every sculpture resembles the human body with minor deviations such as a 3rd leg or hand being present. Sometimes sculptures disappear from the pedestals and appear in other rooms in different poses, as if they were alive. They were seen by wanderers acting out various scenes from human life.
I think The Maker rearranges them himself. It's his place; of course he would do so. My imagination tells me that he plays with them like dolls. Its a matter of time before I see that. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==Pale Garden== The Pale Garden is a large closed garden with unusual plant-shaped pillars instead of plants. The largest ones reach to the ceiling and split the light flowing from the windows on the wall. All corridors in The Whiteout converge to one of the four entrances. Stuffed into a corner, a piano plays calming music on its own. The melodies are distinct, while simultaneously similar to music you'd find in the Frontrooms. In the middle of the room, there is a pedestal with a book on it that cannot be moved, titled "Thoughts", written by The Maker. None of the people who read it remember a single sentence from it. They could only describe what the book was about.
I recall how fascinating the book was to read. The Maker wrote a great deal about himself inside. Although he was fairly critical of himself, he was nevertheless optimistic. His passion for creating is detailed in the book. How he attempted to transform emptiness into something beautiful. Additionally, The Maker wrote about all of his creations, both successful and unsuccessful, and the lessons they taught him. The narrator described how he enjoys observing beings who appreciate his creations from various angles. After finishing reading the book, I had the impression that he was a kind person I had encountered in a dream but eventually forgot about when I woke up. I had a lot of them recently while sleeping in the long hallway behind the white doors. They were always about this white space that i'm still researching. It was nothing special I could even say petty forgettable. These spaces were full of this "White People" that were wandering around sometimes staying in place and looking in a random corner. Then it was him a happy person that never said anything. It was weird yet comforting, sadly most of this experience was forgotten. There were a lot of empty pages that could be filled with text. I think that the more The Whiteout expands, the more thoughts will be written. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==The Maker== The Whiteout has been observed being supernaturally manipulated by one enigmatic individual. There are recordings of him moving between walls and then vanishing after being seen. Although only one face is being used at the moment, this entity is a humanoid being with five faces surrounding his head. He is dressed in a floor-length suit and a white hat. The Maker dislikes being filmed or photographed, and the images are usually became faded soon after. He was formerly known as the Man in White, but when explorers began referring to him as The Maker, he was given a new name that better reflects his true nature. Judging by one of his statements and what was supposedly written in Pale Garden's book, we came to a conclusion that he could be the creator of The Whiteout. Everything from the walls, furniture and sculptures to the anomalies found here are all his creations. The Maker is an artist who expresses his love for creating throughout everything you can see around you in The Whiteout. ===Behavior=== The Maker poses no threat to humans. He doesn't care about their appearance in the level, but sometimes, when in close contact, he slightly waves his hand to greet them before disapearing. He very rarely communicates with people that are in The Whiteout. His voice is comparable to the low, trained voice of a singer.
I don't think I have enough power to continue the research inside The Whiteout. I was running around trying to find him, trying to know the truth, but he always got away. At one point The Maker even blocked my way with a quickly made sculpture titled "Unnecessary". Then it hit me. Maybe I should stop? The truth is that I forgot about one thing: to appreciate this place. There's rarely a safe spot inside a reality like this one. I sat down on a chair and relaxed a bit, though the light would not let me get the whole comfort. I stopped caring about it, though. I looked around; then realised that I was in a sculpture room with a look of beautiful works of art. I said out loud, "It's beautiful." Then the presence of someone started to be felt in my body. After turning my head, I saw a white figure sitting on my left. It was him; it was The Maker. I quickly asked him one question; Who are you? He responded with the words, "Everything that you can imagine." Then magically disappeared. Maybe the only thing he wanted was for someone to appreciate his work. He expands this place in order to make more and more of them. We always run away and try to find a way out, but we never actually appreciate small things like the beauty of this world or the fact that we are alive. Think about that. I do, and I feel better; now I can come back to the settlement. The Whiteout is more close to be explained than some might think. - Ian Marron, Kauer Explorer
==Access== The Whiteout known to be accessible through the doorways found in the [[Level 483|Grayscale Halls]]. There are several dozen of them placed throughout the corridor, all can be recognized by very bright light coming from behind. For many wandering through the long hallways those entrances are important points showing how far they have come. To exit '''The Whiteout''' safely, you should go back to where you came from.
'''See Author'''
